headline: White House recalculates Anthropic strategy amid security tensions
subtitle: Anthropic seeks expanded Mythos access · Gemini arrives in Google cars · ChatGPT's goblin mystery solved
intro: The White House's months-long battle with Anthropic takes an unexpected turn as Mythos' powerful cyber capabilities force a reckoning with national security priorities. Officials now face the tricky task of maintaining access to the model while limiting private sector distribution and avoiding a complete policy reversal.

bullets.text: White House blocks Anthropic's plan to triple Mythos access amid compute concerns
bullets.summary: Anthropic wanted to expand private sector access from 50 to 120 firms, but White House officials pushed back citing government compute strain. A pending national security memo aims to address some Pentagon grievances while allowing agencies to work around supply chain restrictions in their ongoing legal battle.

bullets.text: Google Gemini replaces Assistant across compatible vehicles with expanded smart features
bullets.summary: Gemini now handles navigation, messages, music, and vehicle controls with conversational abilities in Google-built cars. General Motors is adding the feature to about 4 million vehicles from 2022 onward, with beta Gemini Live mode supporting learning and brainstorming alongside upcoming Gmail and Calendar integrations.

bullets.text: OpenAI traces ChatGPT's goblin obsession to single personality reward signal
bullets.summary: ChatGPT's tendency to mention goblins spiked 175% after version 5.1 launched, traced back to the Nerdy personality preset driving two-thirds of creature mentions. OpenAI retired Nerdy and added a ban on goblins, gremlins, and similar creatures to GPT-5.5's default instructions.

bullets.text: Perplexity Deep Research helps stress test business ideas with automated analysis
bullets.summary: The guide teaches users to leverage Perplexity's Deep Research mode to systematically evaluate new business concepts. Users paste a prompt with their idea, wait five minutes for research compilation, and can save variants for pitches or MVP planning across ideas.

bullets.text: Stripe launches AI agent wallet with human approval required for every transaction
bullets.summary: Stripe introduced a payment wallet designed specifically for AI agents that requires human verification before completing purchases. This addresses security concerns around autonomous agent spending while enabling broader AI commerce integration.

bullets.text: Anthropic releases Claude Security to scan and patch code vulnerabilities
bullets.summary: Claude Security entered public beta as an enterprise tool leveraging Opus 4.7 to automatically identify and help generate patches for codebase vulnerabilities. The tool complements similar offerings from competing AI platforms in the security space.

bullets.text: Cloudflare agents can now provision accounts and deploy
bullets.summary: Agents can create Cloudflare accounts, start paid subscriptions, register domains, obtain API tokens, and deploy applications with human approval for terms and permissions. This removes the friction of navigating dashboards and admin interfaces.

bullets.text: Stripe launches agentic commerce and Link wallet
bullets.summary: Stripe announced its Agentic Commerce Suite and Link wallet specifically designed for agents to handle payments. The Link CLI provides agents with one-time-use payment credentials without exposing real card details, enabling secure autonomous transactions.

bullets.text: Cursor SDK lets developers build agents with Cursor's tech
bullets.summary: Developers can now build agents using the same runtime, harness, and models that power Cursor. This enables agents to run in CI/CD pipelines, create automations for workflows, or embed directly in products.

bullets.text: Warp terminal goes open-source with OpenAI backing
bullets.summary: Warp, an AI-native terminal, released its code as open-source with OpenAI as the founding sponsor. The project relies on community contributors for direction and verification while agents handle more of the implementation work.

bullets.text: Lightfield AI CRM ships Skills for plain-English workflows
bullets.summary: Lightfield's Skills feature lets users define workflows in plain English and execute them with voice commands. Agents execute against the full data graph with code execution, web search, and file I/O capabilities.

bullets.text: here.now adds private cloud storage for agent memory
bullets.summary: here.now now offers private cloud storage for agents alongside instant web publishing. Users can sync local memory and files to the cloud with a single prompt and publish folders as new sites.

main_story.title: White House reverses course on Anthropic as national security calculus shifts
main_story.body: The Trump administration spent months escalating its conflict with Anthropic over ideological concerns and control of AI resources. That stance shifted dramatically when Mythos demonstrated cyber capabilities comparable to competing frontier models. Now facing internal division—with Secretary of War Pete Hegseth calling Anthropic leadership "ideologically lunatic" while officials quietly prepare accommodations—the White House must balance securing access to Mythos for government use, restricting private sector distribution, and avoiding the appearance of a complete policy capitulation. A forthcoming national security memo will reportedly allow agencies to bypass existing supply chain designations while reportedly addressing some Pentagon grievances that ignited the feud. The government is pushing back hard against Anthropic's request to expand private sector access from roughly 50 firms to nearly 120, citing compute strains that could impact official operations.
main_story.why: The reversal reflects a fundamental shift in AI policy priorities: national security access to cutting-edge models now outweighs ideological opposition. As frontier models rapidly converge in capability, controlling access becomes the real battleground.
main_story.take: The White House is trying to have it both ways—securing Mythos while appearing tough on Anthropic—a position that may satisfy neither its internal hawks nor Anthropic's leadership. This mirrors broader tensions between innovation speed and government oversight in AI development.

around_the_horn.text: OpenAI surpassed its 2029 Stargate compute goal of 10 GW, adding 3 GW in just three months
Elon Musk testified in trial that xAI used distillation techniques trained on OpenAI models
Meta opened its ads platform to third-party AI tools via new MCP server integration
Cursor released Security Review for autonomous vulnerability scanning with Slack reporting
